items.name: Greece 2004 (Rehhagel)
items.tag_line: Man-marking and a libero anachronism won a European Championship on set pieces and a low block.
items.core_idea: A man-marking, libero-based defence paired with a low block and a relentless set-piece plan won the Euros against every form odds suggested.
items.youth_takeaway: A complete defensive plan, drilled without compromise, can beat teams with far more individual talent.

items.name: Stoke City 2008-12 (Pulis)
items.tag_line: Rory Delap's flat long throws worked as a primary attacking weapon, turning a throw-in into a corner.
items.core_idea: Rory Delap's long, flat throw-ins were treated as a first-class delivery, not a restart, feeding the same second-ball chase as a long ball forward.
items.youth_takeaway: A restart can be a weapon: rehearse the throw-in with the same care as any other delivery.

items.name: Burnley 2016-20 (Dyche)
items.tag_line: A 4-4-2 low block plus an aerial territory game outperformed its budget for years on organisation alone.
items.core_idea: A disciplined 4-4-2 low block combined with a long-ball, aerial-territory approach to consistently outperform a modest budget through organisation.
items.youth_takeaway: Organisation without the ball is a repeatable advantage, not a lesser style of play.

items.name: Wimbledon 1988 (Crazy Gang)
items.tag_line: The original second-ball team: direct play used as open psychological warfare against bigger clubs.
items.core_idea: Direct, physical, long-ball football, played with total conviction, made the second ball the whole game plan against far wealthier opponents.
items.youth_takeaway: Commitment to a simple plan, played without hesitation, can unsettle a more talented opponent.

items.name: Ajax 2018/19 (ten Hag)
items.tag_line: A youth-built positional side pressed Champions League giants off the pitch on press resistance alone.
items.core_idea: A home-grown, positionally trained squad pressed elite European opponents off the ball, with de Jong's press resistance underpinning the entire build-out.
items.youth_takeaway: A youth-development pathway can produce a team that outplays clubs with far larger squads.

items.name: Chile 2014-16 (Sampaoli)
items.tag_line: National-team man-marking pressure chaos delivered back-to-back Copa America titles.
items.core_idea: An all-pitch man-marking press, in the Bielsa lineage, produced back-to-back continental titles built on relentless pressure rather than possession control.
items.youth_takeaway: A clear defensive job for every player can be a title-winning identity on its own.
